---
title: "Retail Price Optimization using Neural Network"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_notebook: default
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r}

# Step 1 : Initial Data Setup
# Import all libraries required
library(readxl)
library(caret)
library(tidyverse)

# Enter file path as required
Retail_Price_Optimization_Dataset <- read_excel("C:/Users/Atharva/OneDrive/Desktop/My_Work/MSc-II-Work/Microsoft Excel/Retail Price Optimization Dataset.xlsx")
# Dataset can be found here :
# https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/suddharshan/retail-price-optimization/data
df = Retail_Price_Optimization_Dataset
# View(df)

```

```{r}

# Step 2 : Data preprocessing, check for missing values
library(skimr)
skim(df)

# Step 2A : Remove unnecessary columns and rename columns wherever required
df_clean = df[, !names(df) %in% c("product_id", "month_year", "product_name_lenght",
                                  "product_description_lenght", "product_photos_qty", "s")]
df_clean = df_clean %>%
  rename(
    # Target and Core Price
    monthly_sales_qty = qty, monthly_revenue = total_price,
    company_unit_price = unit_price, previous_month_price = lag_price,
    
    # Company Logistics/Features
    company_freight_price = freight_price, product_weight_grams = product_weight_g,
    
    # Competitor 1
    comp1_price = comp_1, comp1_score = ps1, comp1_freight_price = fp1,
    
    # Competitor 2
    comp2_price = comp_2, comp2_score = ps2, comp2_freight_price = fp2,
    
    # Competitor 3
    comp3_price = comp_3, comp3_score = ps3, comp3_freight_price = fp3
  )

# Step 2B : One-Hot Encoding of product_category_name
# This step is done separately for clarity, and because inputs in NN must be numeric
ohe_product_categories = dummyVars(~ product_category_name, data = df_clean)
ohe_data_matrix = predict(ohe_product_categories, newdata = df_clean)
df_ohe = as_tibble(ohe_data_matrix)
df_ohe = df_ohe %>%
  rename_with(~ str_replace(., "product_category_name", ""))

df_clean = df_clean %>%
  select(-product_category_name) %>%
  bind_cols(df_ohe)

# Cleaned dataset for analysis
skim(df_clean)

```

```{r}

# Step 3 : Implementation of Neural Network (NN) (Regression Problem)
library(keras)
library(tensorflow)
library(ggplot2)

# Step 3A : Regression Modeling

# Set seed for reproducibility with TensorFlow (through Keras)
library(reticulate)
if(requireNamespace("reticulate", quietly = TRUE)){
  reticulate::py_set_seed(123)
}
tf$random$set_seed(123)

# Inputs as well as output are normalized for a NN to work efficiently
target_col = "monthly_revenue"
input_cols = names(df_clean)[!names(df_clean) %in% target_col]

# Regression Model Fit to check statistically significant features
predictor_string = paste(input_cols, collapse = " + ")
formula_string = paste(target_col, "~", predictor_string)
reg_formula = as.formula(formula_string)
reg_model = lm(reg_formula, data = df_clean);reg_model
model_summary = summary(reg_model);model_summary

# Check if any columns have negative values
# Useful when choosing activation functions
negative_check = sapply(df_clean[, input_cols], function(x) any(x < 0))
negative_cols = names(negative_check[negative_check == TRUE]);negative_cols

```

```{r}

# Step 3B : Hyperparameter Setup
# Normalization of input columns using Min-Max scaling in [0, 1]
preproc_params = preProcess(df_clean[, c(target_col, input_cols)], method = c("range"))
df_normalized = predict(preproc_params, df_clean)

# Data Splitting
set.seed(123)
index = createDataPartition(df_normalized[[target_col]], p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_data <- df_normalized[index, ]
test_data <- df_normalized[-index, ]

X_train = as.matrix(train_data[, input_cols])
Y_train = as.matrix(train_data[[target_col]])
X_test = as.matrix(test_data[, input_cols])
Y_test = test_data[[target_col]]

# Input shape for Keras models
input_shape = ncol(X_train)
epochs_run = 50 # number of iterations

```

```{r}

# Step 3C : Function Definition for Model Performance
evaluate_regression_model = function(model, X_test, Y_test_scaled, preproc_params, target_col){
  predictions_scaled = predict(model, X_test)
  predictions_vector = as.vector(predictions_scaled)
  
  # Metrics for regression model performance
  
  # Coefficient of Determination (R^2)
  SST = sum((Y_test_scaled - mean(Y_test_scaled))^2)
  SSE = sum((Y_test_scaled - predictions_vector)^2)
  R_squared = 1 - (SSE/SST)
  
  # Root Mean Square Error (RMSE)
  RMSE = sqrt(mean((Y_test_scaled - predictions_vector)^2))
  
  # Mean Absolute Error (MAE)
  MAE = mean(abs(Y_test_scaled - predictions_vector))
  
  # Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE)
  actual_df_input = as_tibble(X_test)
  actual_df_input[[target_col]] = Y_test_scaled
  predictions_df_input = as_tibble(X_test)
  predictions_df_input[[target_col]] = predictions_vector
  Y_test_original_full = predict(preproc_params, actual_df_input)
  predictions_original_full = predict(preproc_params, predictions_df_input)
  Y_test_original = Y_test_original_full[[target_col]]
  predictions_original = predictions_original_full[[target_col]]
  
  # To avoid zero division error
  epsilon = 1e-8 # tolerance
  MAPE = mean(abs((Y_test_original - predictions_original)/(Y_test_original + epsilon)))*100
  
  return(list(R_squared = R_squared, RMSE = RMSE, MAE = MAE, MAPE = MAPE))
}

```

```{r}

# Step 4 : Model Training and Evaluation

# Model 1 : With Sigmoid Activation Function
nn_model_sigmoid = keras_model_sequential() %>%
  # Input Layer : 31 neurons (22 features + 9 one-hot encoded features)
  layer_dense(units = 31, activation = "sigmoid", input_shape = input_shape) %>%
  # Hidden Layer 1 : 32 neurons
  layer_dense(units = 32, activation = "sigmoid") %>%
  # Hidden Layer 2 : 16 neurons
  layer_dense(units = 16, activation = "sigmoid") %>%
  # Output Layer : 1 neuron
  layer_dense(units = 1, activation = "linear")
nn_model_sigmoid %>% compile(optimizer = "adam", loss = "mse", metrics = c("mae"))

history_sigmoid = nn_model_sigmoid %>%
  fit(X_train, Y_train, epochs = epochs_run, batch_size = 32, validation_split = 0.2, verbose = 0)

metrics_sigmoid = evaluate_regression_model(nn_model_sigmoid, X_test, Y_test, preproc_params, target_col)

# Model 2 : With Rectified Linear Unit (ReLU) Activation Function
nn_model_relu = keras_model_sequential() %>%
  # Input Layer : 31 neurons (22 features + 9 one-hot encoded features)
  layer_dense(units = 31, activation = "relu", input_shape = input_shape) %>%
  # Hidden Layer 1 : 32 neurons
  layer_dense(units = 32, activation = "relu") %>%
  # Hidden Layer 2 : 16 neurons
  layer_dense(units = 16, activation = "relu") %>%
  # Output Layer : 1 neuron
  layer_dense(units = 1, activation = "linear")
nn_model_relu %>% compile(optimizer = "adam", loss = "mse", metrics = c("mae"))

history_relu = nn_model_relu %>%
  fit(X_train, Y_train, epochs = epochs_run, batch_size = 32, validation_split = 0.2, verbose = 0)

metrics_relu = evaluate_regression_model(nn_model_relu, X_test, Y_test, preproc_params, target_col)

# Model 3 : With Leaky ReLU Activation Function
nn_model_leakyrelu = keras_model_sequential() %>%
  # Input Layer : 31 neurons (22 features + 9 one-hot encoded features)
  layer_dense(units = 31, input_shape = input_shape) %>%
  layer_activation_leaky_relu(alpha = 0.01) %>%
  # Hidden Layer 1 : 32 neurons
  layer_dense(units = 32) %>%
  layer_activation_leaky_relu(alpha = 0.01) %>%
  # Hidden Layer 2 : 16 neurons
  layer_dense(units = 16) %>%
  layer_activation_leaky_relu(alpha = 0.01) %>%
  # Output Layer : 1 neuron
  layer_dense(units = 1, activation = "linear")
nn_model_leakyrelu %>% compile(optimizer = "adam", loss = "mse", metrics = c("mae"))

history_leakyrelu = nn_model_leakyrelu %>%
  fit(X_train, Y_train, epochs = epochs_run, batch_size = 32, validation_split = 0.2, verbose = 0)

metrics_leakyrelu = evaluate_regression_model(nn_model_leakyrelu, X_test, Y_test, preproc_params, target_col)

```

```{r}

# Step 5 : Model Comparison and Visualization

# Step 5A : Visualization of Training History
# Plot of training loss vs validation loss

# Number of epochs recorded (same for all three models, so we can choose any one here)
num_epochs = length(history_relu$metrics$loss);num_epochs

history_sigmoid_df = as.data.frame(history_sigmoid$metrics) %>%
  mutate(Model = "Sigmoid", Epoch = 1:num_epochs)
history_relu_df = as.data.frame(history_relu$metrics) %>%
  mutate(Model = "ReLU", Epoch = 1:num_epochs)
history_leakyrelu_df = as.data.frame(history_leakyrelu$metrics) %>%
  mutate(Model = "Leaky ReLU", Epoch = 1:num_epochs)
all_history = bind_rows(history_sigmoid_df, history_relu_df, history_leakyrelu_df)

plot_history = all_history %>%
  select(Model, Epoch, loss, val_loss) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c("loss", "val_loss"),
               names_to = "Metric_Type", values_to = "Loss_Value") %>%
  mutate(Metric_Type = case_match(Metric_Type,
                                  "loss" ~ "Training Loss (MSE)",
                                  "val_loss" ~ "Validation Loss (MSE)"))
ggplot(plot_history, aes(x = Epoch, y = Loss_Value, color = Model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ Metric_Type, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Neural Network Loss Curves by Activation Function",
       y = "Mean Squared Error (MSE)", x = "Epoch") +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

```

```{r}

# Step 5B : Model Comparison
model_comparison = tibble(
  Function = c("Sigmoid", "ReLU", "Leaky ReLU"),
  R2 = c(metrics_sigmoid$R_squared, metrics_relu$R_squared, metrics_leakyrelu$R_squared),
  RMSE = c(metrics_sigmoid$RMSE, metrics_relu$RMSE, metrics_leakyrelu$RMSE),
  MAE = c(metrics_sigmoid$MAE, metrics_relu$MAE, metrics_leakyrelu$MAE),
  MAPE = c(metrics_sigmoid$MAPE, metrics_relu$MAPE, metrics_leakyrelu$MAPE)
)
print(model_comparison %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(., 4))), n = 3, width = Inf)

```

```{r}

# Step 5C : Visualization
plot_data = model_comparison %>%
  select(Function, R2, RMSE, MAE, MAPE) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("R") | starts_with("M"),
               names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Value") %>%
  mutate(Metric = case_match(Metric, "R2" ~ "R-squared (R2)", "RMSE" ~ "RMSE (scaled)", "MAE" ~ "MAE (scaled)", "MAPE" ~ "MAPE (%)"))
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Function, y = Value, fill = Function)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  facet_wrap(~ Metric, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Neural Network Performance by Activation Function",
       y = "Metric Value", x = "Activation Function") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

```

Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Ctrl+Alt+I*.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Ctrl+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.
